hadith-eng: Narrated Kuraib Maula Ibn `Abbas:`Abdullah bin `Abbas said that he had passed a night in the house of Maimuna the mother of the faithful believers , who was his aunt. He said, "I slept across the bed, and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) along with his wife slept lengthwise. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) slept till midnight or slightly before or after it. Then Allah's Apostle woke up, sat, and removed the traces of sleep by rubbing his hands over his face. Then he recited the last ten verses of Surat-Al `Imran (2). Then he went towards a hanging leather watercontainer and performed a perfect ablution and then stood up for prayer." `Abdullah bin `Abbas added, "I got up and did the same as Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had done and then went and stood by his side. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) then put his right hand over my head and caught my right ear and twisted it. He offered two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at and then offered one rak`a witr. Then he lay down till the Mu'adh-dhin came and then he prayed two light rak`at and went out and offered the early morning (Fajr) prayer
question: What actions did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) perform upon waking up in the middle of the night?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the Prophet Muhammad's (ﷺ) actions upon waking up in the middle of the night, including performing ablution, prayer, and witr.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:We used to greet the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was praying and he used to answer our greetings. When we returned from An-Najashi (the ruler of Ethiopia), we greeted him, but he did not answer us (during the prayer) and (after finishing the prayer) he said, "In the prayer one is occupied (with a more serious matter)
question: What is the significance of maintaining focus and avoiding distractions during prayer in Islam?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of maintaining focus during prayer by highlighting the Prophet's behavior when greeting him during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Arqam:In the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) we used to speak while praying, and one of us would tell his needs to his companions, till the verse, 'Guard strictly your prayers (2.238) was revealed. After that we were ordered to remain silent while praying
question: What changes were made to the conduct of prayer after the revelation of a specific verse in the Quran?
verification_reason: The answer explains the change in conduct of prayer due to the revelation of a specific verse.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The Prophet (ﷺ) went out to affect a reconciliation between the tribes of Bani `Amr bin `Auf and the time of the prayer became due; Bilal went to Abu Bakr and said, "The Prophet (ﷺ) is detained. Will you lead the people in the prayer?" Abu Bakr replied, "Yes, if you wish." So Bilal pronounced the Iqama and Abu Bakr led the prayer. In the meantime the Prophet (ﷺ) came crossing the rows (of the praying people) till he stood in the first row and the people started clapping. Abu Bakr never looked hither and thither during the prayer but when the people clapped too much, he looked back and saw the Prophet (ﷺ) in the (first) row. The Prophet (ﷺ) waved him to remain at his place, but Abu Bakr raised both his hands and sent praises to Allah and then retreated and the Prophet (ﷺ) went forward and led the prayer. (See Hadith No)
question: What actions should be taken if the designated prayer leader is absent when the prayer time arrives?
verification_reason: The Hadith text describes a situation where the designated prayer leader (Abu Bakr) is asked to lead the prayer when the Prophet is absent, and it is confirmed that Abu Bakr leads the prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud:We used to say the greeting, name and greet each other in the prayer. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) heard it and said:--"Say, 'at-tahiyyatu lil-lahi was-salawatu wat-taiyibatu . Assalamu 'Alaika aiyuha-n-Nabiyu warahmatu- l-lahi wa-barakatuhu. _ Assalamu alaina wa-'ala 'ibadi-l-lahi as-salihin.. Ashhadu an la ilaha illa-l-lah wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan `Abdu hu wa Rasuluh. (All the compliments are for Allah and all the prayers and all the good things (are for Allah). Peace be on you, O Prophet, and Allah's mercy and blessings (are on you). And peace be on us and on the good (pious) worshipers of Allah. I testify that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is His slave and Apostle.) So, when you have said this, then you have surely sent the greetings to every good (pious) worshipper of Allah, whether he be in the Heaven or on the Earth
question: What is the significance of the specific phrases used in the Tashahhud during prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of the specific phrases used in the Tashahhud during prayer, as instructed by the Prophet Muhammad.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The saying 'Subhan Allah' is for men and clapping is for women." (If something happens in the prayer, the men can invite the attention of the Imam by saying "Subhan Allah". And women, by clapping their hands)
question: What is the prescribed way for men to alert the Imam during prayer if something happens?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing the prescribed way for men to alert the Imam during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The saying 'Subhan Allah' is for men and clapping is for women
question: What is the significance of saying 'Subhan Allah' in Islamic practice?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the significance of 'Subhan Allah' in Islamic practice, specifically in the context of expressing joy or gratitude.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik: While Abu Bakr was leading the people in the morning prayer on a Monday, the Prophet (ﷺ) came towards them suddenly having lifted the curtain of 'Aisha's house, and looked at them as they were standing in rows and smiled. Abu Bakr tried to come back thinking that Allah's Apostle wanted to come out for the prayer. The attention of the Muslims was diverted from the prayer because they were delighted to see the Prophet. The Prophet (ﷺ) waved his hand to them to complete their prayer, then he went back into the room and let down the curtain. The Prophet expired on that very day
question: What significance does the Prophet's smile hold for the companions during the prayer?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of the Prophet's smile during prayer, which was a distraction for the companions.

hadith-eng: Narrated Mu'aiqib:The Prophet (ﷺ) talked about a man leveling the earth on prostrating, and said, "If you have to do so, then do it once
question: What guidance did the Prophet (ﷺ) provide regarding leveling the earth during prostration?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the Prophet's guidance on leveling the earth during prostration.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:We used to pray with the Prophet (ﷺ) in scorching heat, and if someone of us could not put his face on the earth (because of the heat) then he would spread his clothes and prostrate over them
question: How did the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) adapt their prayer practices in extreme weather conditions?
verification_reason: The answer describes a specific scenario where companions adapted their prayer practices in extreme weather conditions (scorching heat).

hadith-eng: Narrated Aisha:I used to stretch my legs towards the Qibla of the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was praying; whenever he prostrated he touched me, and I would withdraw my legs, and whenever he stood up, I would restretch my legs
question: What does this narration indicate about the interaction between the Prophet (ﷺ) and his family during prayer?
verification_reason: The narration describes the Prophet's (ﷺ) interaction with his family during prayer, specifically how he would touch the narrator's legs while prostrating.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) once offered the prayer and said, "Satan came in front of me and tried to interrupt my prayer, but Allah gave me an upper hand on him and I choked him. No doubt, I thought of tying him to one of the pillars of the mosque till you get up in the morning and see him. Then I remembered the statement of Prophet Solomon, 'My Lord ! Bestow on me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me.' Then Allah made him (Satan) return with his head down (humiliated)
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) deal with distractions from Satan during prayer?
verification_reason: The text describes the Prophet's experience with Satan during prayer and how Allah helped him overcome the distraction.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Azraq bin Qais:We were at Al-Ahwaz fighting the Al-Haruriya (tribe). While I was at the bank of a river a man was praying and the reins of his animal were in his hands and the animal was struggling and he was following the animal. (Shu`ba, a sub-narrator, said that man was Abu Barza Al-Aslami). A man from the Khawarij said, "O Allah! Be harsh to this sheik." And when the sheik (Abu Barza) finished his prayer, he said, "I heard your remark. No doubt, I participated with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in six or seven or eight holy battles and saw his leniency, and no doubt, I would rather retain my animal than let it return to its stable, as it would cause me much trouble
question: What can be learned about the balance between religious duties and practical responsibilities from this incident?
verification_reason: The incident illustrates the balance between fulfilling religious duties (praying) and practical responsibilities (taking care of one's animal).

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:Once the sun eclipsed and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) stood up for the prayer and recited a very long Sura and then bowed for a long while and then raised his head and started reciting another Sura. Then he bowed, and after finishing, he prostrated and did the same in the second rak`a and then said, "These (lunar and solar eclipses) are two of the signs of Allah and if you see them, pray till the eclipse is over. No doubt, while standing at this place I saw everything promised to me by Allah and I saw (Paradise) and I wanted to pluck a bunch (of grapes) therefrom, at the time when you saw me stepping forward. No doubt, I saw Hell with its different parts destroying each other when you saw me retreating and in it I saw `Amr bin Luhai who started the tradition of freeing animals (set them free) in the name of idols
question: What is the significance of performing prayer during a solar or lunar eclipse in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of performing prayer during a solar or lunar eclipse, as instructed by the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) saw some sputum on the wall facing the Qibla of the mosque and became furious with the people of the mosque and said, "During the prayer, Allah is in front of everyone of you and so he should not spit (or said, 'He should not expectorate')." Then he got down and scratched the sputum with his hand. Ibn `Umar said (after narrating), "If anyone of you has to spit during the prayer, he should spit to his left
question: What is the significance of maintaining cleanliness and respect in the mosque during prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of maintaining cleanliness and respect in the mosque during prayer by referencing a Hadith that emphasizes the importance of not spitting or expectorating during prayer and provides guidance on how to handle it if necessary.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whenever anyone of you is in prayer, he is speaking in private to his Lord and so he should neither spit in front of him nor on his right side but to his left side under his left foot
question: What is the significance of maintaining cleanliness and etiquette during prayer?
verification_reason: The answer explains a specific etiquette to maintain during prayer, which is related to the question about the significance of cleanliness and etiquette during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The people used to offer the prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) with their waist-sheets tied round their necks because of the shortness of the sheets and the women were ordered not to lift their heads till the men had sat straight
question: What were the clothing practices of men during prayer in the time of the Prophet (ﷺ)?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about clothing practices of men during prayer in the time of the Prophet (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:I used to greet the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was in prayer and he would return my greeting, but when we returned (from Ethiopia) I greeted the Prophet (while he was praying) but he did not return the greeting, and (after finishing the prayer) he said, "In the prayer one is occupied (with a more serious matter)
question: What is the significance of maintaining focus and concentration during prayer in Islam?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of maintaining focus and concentration during prayer by highlighting the Prophet's behavior when greeting him while he was praying.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The news about the differences amongst the people of Bani `Amr bin `Auf at Quba reached Allah's Apostle and so he went to them along with some of his companions to affect a reconciliation. Allah's Apostle was delayed there and the time for the prayer became due. Bilal came to Abu Bakr and said, "O Abu Bakr! Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) is detained (there) and the time for the prayer is due. Will you lead the people in prayer?" Abu Bakr replied, "Yes, if you wish." So Bilal pronounced the Iqama and Abu Bakr went forward and the people said Takbir. In the meantime, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came piercing through the rows till he stood in the (first) row and the people started clapping. Abu Bakr, would never look hither and thither during the prayer but when the people clapped much he looked back and saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ). The Prophet (ﷺ) beckoned him to carry on. Abu Bakr raised both his hands, praised Allah and retreated till he stood in the row and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) went forward and led the people in the prayer. When he had finished the prayer, he addressed the people and said, "O people! Why did you start clapping when something happened to you in the prayer? Clapping is for women. Whenever one is confronted with something unusual in the prayer one should say, 'Subhan Allah'." Then the Prophet looked towards Abu Bakr and asked, "What prevented you from leading the prayer when I beckoned you to carry on?" Abu Bakr replied, "It does not befit the son of Al Quhafa to lead the prayer in the presence of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)
question: What actions should be taken if the designated prayer leader is delayed and the prayer time becomes due?
verification_reason: The text explains the correct action to take when the designated prayer leader is delayed and the prayer time becomes due, which is for another person to lead the prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:It was forbidden to pray with the hands over one's hips
question: What is the ruling on the position of hands during prayer in Islam?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the question about the position of hands during prayer in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Uqba bin Al-Harith:I offered the `Asr prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and after finishing the prayer with Taslim he got up quickly and went to some of his wives and then came out. He noticed the signs of astonishment on the faces of the people caused by his speed. He then said, "I remembered while I was in my prayer that a piece of gold was Lying in my house and I disliked that it should remain with us throughout the night, and so I have ordered it to be distributed
question: What actions did the Prophet (ﷺ) take upon remembering something during prayer?
verification_reason: The answer describes the Prophet's actions upon remembering something during prayer, which is what the question asks.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "When the Adhan for the prayer is pronounced, then Satan takes to his heels passing wind so that he may not hear the Adhan and when the Mu'adh-dhin finishes, he comes back; and when the Iqama is pronounced he again takes to his heels and when it is finished, he again comes back and continues reminding the praying person of things that he used not to remember when not in prayer till he forgets how much he has prayed." Abu Salama bin `Abdur-Rahman said, "If anyone of you has such a thing (forgetting the number of rak`at he has prayed) he should perform two prostrations of Sahu (i.e. forgetfulness) while sitting." Abu Salama narrates this from Abu Huraira
question: What actions does Satan take during the Adhan and Iqama, and how does it affect the person praying?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes Satan's behavior during Adhan and Iqama and its effect on the person praying.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:People say that I narrate too many narrations of the Prophet; once I met a man (during the lifetime of the Prophet) and asked him, "Which Sura did Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) recite yesterday in the `Isha' prayer?" He said, "I do not know." I said, "Did you not attend the prayer?" He said, "Yes, (I did)." I said, "I know. He recited such and such Sura
question: What can be inferred about the importance of attentiveness during prayers from this narration?
verification_reason: The narration implies that the narrator is emphasizing the importance of attentiveness during prayers by highlighting the man's lack of knowledge about the recited Surah despite attending the prayer.

Will you lead the people in prayer?" Abu Bakr replied, "Yes, if you wish." So Bilal pronounced the Iqama and Abu Bakr went forward and the people said Takbir. In the meantime, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came piercing through the rows till he stood in the (first) row and the people started clapping. Abu Bakr, would never look hither and thither during the prayer but when the people clapped much he looked back and saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ). The Prophet (ﷺ) beckoned him to carry on. Abu Bakr raised both his hands, praised Allah and retreated till he stood in the row and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) went forward and led the people in the prayer. When he had finished the prayer, he addressed the people and said, "O people! Why did you start clapping when something happened to you in the prayer? Clapping is for women. Whenever one is confronted with something unusual in the prayer one should say, 'Subhan Allah'." Then the Prophet looked towards Abu Bakr and asked, "What prevented you from leading the prayer when I beckoned you to carry on?" Abu Bakr replied, "It does not befit the son of Al Quhafa to lead the prayer in the presence of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)
question: How should one react during prayer if something unusual occurs?
verification_reason: The answer explains the correct behavior during prayer when something unusual occurs, which is to say 'Sub Han Allah' and continue with the prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:It was forbidden to keep the hands on the hips during the prayer. (This is narrated by Abu Huraira from the Prophet)

hadith-eng: Narrated `Uqba bin Al-Harith:I offered the `Asr prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) and after finishing the prayer with Taslim he got up quickly and went to some of his wives and then came out. He noticed the signs of astonishment on the faces of the people caused by his speed. He then said, "I remembered while I was in my prayer that a piece of gold was Lying in my house and I disliked that it should remain with us throughout the night, and so I have ordered it to be distributed
question: How did the Prophet (ﷺ) demonstrate his concern for not keeping wealth overnight?
verification_reason: The text explains the Prophet's (ﷺ) concern for not keeping wealth overnight by showing his immediate action to distribute the gold.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "When the Adhan for the prayer is pronounced, then Satan takes to his heels passing wind so that he may not hear the Adhan and when the Mu'adh-dhin finishes, he comes back; and when the Iqama is pronounced he again takes to his heels and when it is finished, he again comes back and continues reminding the praying person of things that he used not to remember when not in prayer till he forgets how much he has prayed." Abu Salama bin `Abdur-Rahman said, "If anyone of you has such a thing (forgetting the number of rak`at he has prayed) he should perform two prostrations of Sahu (i.e. forgetfulness) while sitting." Abu Salama narrates this from Abu Huraira
question: How can a person address forgetfulness during prayer according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear explanation of how Satan affects a person's memory during prayer and how to address forgetfulness by performing two prostrations of Sahu.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:People say that I narrate too many narrations of the Prophet; once I met a man (during the lifetime of the Prophet) and asked him, "Which Sura did Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) recite yesterday in the `Isha' prayer?" He said, "I do not know." I said, "Did you not attend the prayer?" He said, "Yes, (I did)." I said, "I know. He recited such and such Sura
question: How does this narration highlight the significance of remembering and recalling the recitations in prayers?
verification_reason: The narration highlights the significance of remembering and recalling the recitations in prayers by showing the narrator's ability to recall the Surah recited by the Prophet in the previous night's prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Kuraib Maula Ibn `Abbas:`Abdullah bin `Abbas said that he had passed a night in the house of Maimuna the mother of the faithful believers , who was his aunt. He said, "I slept across the bed, and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) along with his wife slept lengthwise. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) slept till midnight or slightly before or after it. Then Allah's Apostle woke up, sat, and removed the traces of sleep by rubbing his hands over his face. Then he recited the last ten verses of Surat-Al `Imran (2). Then he went towards a hanging leather watercontainer and performed a perfect ablution and then stood up for prayer." `Abdullah bin `Abbas added, "I got up and did the same as Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) had done and then went and stood by his side. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) then put his right hand over my head and caught my right ear and twisted it. He offered two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at, then two rak`at and then offered one rak`a witr. Then he lay down till the Mu'adh-dhin came and then he prayed two light rak`at and went out and offered the early morning (Fajr) prayer
question: What is the significance of performing witr prayer according to this narration?
verification_reason: The narration describes the performance of witr prayer by the Prophet (ﷺ) as part of his night prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:We used to greet the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was praying and he used to answer our greetings. When we returned from An-Najashi (the ruler of Ethiopia), we greeted him, but he did not answer us (during the prayer) and (after finishing the prayer) he said, "In the prayer one is occupied (with a more serious matter)
question: What changes occurred in the Prophet's practice of responding to greetings during prayer after returning from An-Najashi?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the change in the Prophet's practice of responding to greetings during prayer after returning from An-Najashi.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Arqam:In the lifetime of the Prophet (ﷺ) we used to speak while praying, and one of us would tell his needs to his companions, till the verse, 'Guard strictly your prayers (2.238) was revealed. After that we were ordered to remain silent while praying
question: What is the significance of maintaining silence during prayer in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of maintaining silence during prayer in Islam, citing a relevant verse and its impact on the Prophet's companions.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The saying 'Subhan Allah' is for men and clapping is for women." (If something happens in the prayer, the men can invite the attention of the Imam by saying "Subhan Allah". And women, by clapping their hands)

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik: While Abu Bakr was leading the people in the morning prayer on a Monday, the Prophet (ﷺ) came towards them suddenly having lifted the curtain of 'Aisha's house, and looked at them as they were standing in rows and smiled. Abu Bakr tried to come back thinking that Allah's Apostle wanted to come out for the prayer. The attention of the Muslims was diverted from the prayer because they were delighted to see the Prophet. The Prophet (ﷺ) waved his hand to them to complete their prayer, then he went back into the room and let down the curtain. The Prophet expired on that very day
question: What actions did the Prophet take when he observed the prayer being led by Abu Bakr?
verification_reason: The text describes the Prophet's actions when he observed Abu Bakr leading the prayer, which matches the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "A woman called her son while he was in his hermitage and said, 'O Juraij' He said, 'O Allah, my mother (is calling me) and (I am offering) my prayer (what shall I do)?' She again said, 'O Juraij!' He said again, 'O Allah ! My mother (is calling me) and (I am offering) my prayer (what shall I do)?' She again said, 'O Juraij' He again said, 'O Allah! My mother (is calling me) and (I am offering) my prayer. (What shall I do?)' She said, 'O Allah! Do not let Juraij die till he sees the faces of prostitutes.' A shepherdess used to come by his hermitage for grazing her sheep and she gave birth to a child. She was asked whose child that was, and she replied that it was from Juraij and that he had come out from his hermitage. Juraij said, 'Where is that woman who claims that her child is from me?' (When she was brought to him along with the child), Juraij asked the child, 'O Babus, who is your father?' The child replied, 'The shepherd.' " (See Hadith No 662. Vol)
question: What lessons can be learned from the story of Juraij regarding the power of a mother's supplication?
verification_reason: The answer text provides a story that illustrates the power of a mother's supplication, as requested in the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated Mu'aiqib:The Prophet (ﷺ) talked about a man leveling the earth on prostrating, and said, "If you have to do so, then do it once
question: What is the significance of minimizing actions during prayer as taught by the Prophet (ﷺ)?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a specific example of minimizing actions during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:We used to pray with the Prophet (ﷺ) in scorching heat, and if someone of us could not put his face on the earth (because of the heat) then he would spread his clothes and prostrate over them
question: What methods were used by the companions to perform prostration when the ground was too hot?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing a method used by the companions to perform prostration when the ground was too hot.

hadith-eng: Narrated Aisha:I used to stretch my legs towards the Qibla of the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was praying; whenever he prostrated he touched me, and I would withdraw my legs, and whenever he stood up, I would restretch my legs
question: What can be inferred about the flexibility and adaptability in prayer settings from this narration?
verification_reason: The narration shows the flexibility and adaptability of the Prophet (ﷺ) in prayer settings, allowing a child to be close to him.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) once offered the prayer and said, "Satan came in front of me and tried to interrupt my prayer, but Allah gave me an upper hand on him and I choked him. No doubt, I thought of tying him to one of the pillars of the mosque till you get up in the morning and see him. Then I remembered the statement of Prophet Solomon, 'My Lord ! Bestow on me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me.' Then Allah made him (Satan) return with his head down (humiliated)
question: What does this narration teach about the power and protection granted by Allah against Satanic influences?
verification_reason: The narration explicitly describes the Prophet's experience with Satan's attempt to interrupt his prayer and Allah's protection and power over Satan.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Azraq bin Qais:We were at Al-Ahwaz fighting the Al-Haruriya (tribe). While I was at the bank of a river a man was praying and the reins of his animal were in his hands and the animal was struggling and he was following the animal. (Shu`ba, a sub-narrator, said that man was Abu Barza Al-Aslami). A man from the Khawarij said, "O Allah! Be harsh to this sheik." And when the sheik (Abu Barza) finished his prayer, he said, "I heard your remark. No doubt, I participated with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in six or seven or eight holy battles and saw his leniency, and no doubt, I would rather retain my animal than let it return to its stable, as it would cause me much trouble
question: What does this narration reveal about the attitudes of the Khawarij towards other Muslims?
verification_reason: The narration reveals the Khawarij's harsh attitude towards Abu Barza Al-Aslami, as they wished Allah to be harsh to him.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:Once the sun eclipsed and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) stood up for the prayer and recited a very long Sura and then bowed for a long while and then raised his head and started reciting another Sura. Then he bowed, and after finishing, he prostrated and did the same in the second rak`a and then said, "These (lunar and solar eclipses) are two of the signs of Allah and if you see them, pray till the eclipse is over. No doubt, while standing at this place I saw everything promised to me by Allah and I saw (Paradise) and I wanted to pluck a bunch (of grapes) therefrom, at the time when you saw me stepping forward. No doubt, I saw Hell with its different parts destroying each other when you saw me retreating and in it I saw `Amr bin Luhai who started the tradition of freeing animals (set them free) in the name of idols
question: Who was `Amr bin Luhai and what was his role in pre-Islamic traditions?
verification_reason: The text correctly answers the question by mentioning `Amr bin Luhai's role in pre-Islamic traditions, specifically starting the tradition of freeing animals in the name of idols.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) saw some sputum on the wall facing the Qibla of the mosque and became furious with the people of the mosque and said, "During the prayer, Allah is in front of everyone of you and so he should not spit (or said, 'He should not expectorate')." Then he got down and scratched the sputum with his hand. Ibn `Umar said (after narrating), "If anyone of you has to spit during the prayer, he should spit to his left
question: What does the reaction of the Prophet (ﷺ) to seeing sputum in the mosque teach about the importance of etiquette in places of worship?
verification_reason: The answer explains the Prophet's reaction to sputum in the mosque and provides guidance on etiquette during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whenever anyone of you is in prayer, he is speaking in private to his Lord and so he should neither spit in front of him nor on his right side but to his left side under his left foot
question: What are the recommended actions if one needs to spit during prayer?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a specific instruction from the Prophet (ﷺ) regarding spitting during prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The people used to offer the prayer with the Prophet (ﷺ) with their waist-sheets tied round their necks because of the shortness of the sheets and the women were ordered not to lift their heads till the men had sat straight
question: How did the shortness of clothing affect the way people dressed for prayer in early Islamic times?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the effect of shortness of clothing on the way people dressed for prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:I used to greet the Prophet (ﷺ) while he was in prayer and he would return my greeting, but when we returned (from Ethiopia) I greeted the Prophet (while he was praying) but he did not return the greeting, and (after finishing the prayer) he said, "In the prayer one is occupied (with a more serious matter)

verification_reason: The answer highlights the change in practice regarding greetings during prayer, as the Prophet (ﷺ) used to return greetings before but not after returning from Ethiopia.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) sent me for some job and when I had finished it I returned and came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and greeted him but he did not return my greeting. So I felt so sorry that only Allah knows it and I said to myself,, 'Perhaps Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) is angry because I did not come quickly, then again I greeted him but he did not reply. I felt even more sorry than I did the first time. Again I greeted him and he returned the greeting and said, "The thing which prevented me from returning the greeting was that I was praying." And at that time he was on his Rahila and his face was not towards the Qibla

verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of facing the Qibla during prayer and mentions an exception to the rule (not facing the Qibla while on a Rahila)

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The news about the differences amongst the people of Bani `Amr bin `Auf at Quba reached Allah's Apostle and so he went to them along with some of his companions to affect a reconciliation. Allah's Apostle was delayed there and the time for the prayer became due. Bilal came to Abu Bakr and said, "O Abu Bakr! Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) is detained (there) and the time for the prayer is due. Will you lead the people in prayer?" Abu Bakr replied, "Yes, if you wish." So Bilal pronounced the Iqama and Abu Bakr went forward and the people said Takbir. In the meantime, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came piercing through the rows till he stood in the (first) row and the people started clapping. Abu Bakr, would never look hither and thither during the prayer but when the people clapped much he looked back and saw Allah's Messenger (ﷺ). The Prophet (ﷺ) beckoned him to carry on. Abu Bakr raised both his hands, praised Allah and retreated till he stood in the row and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) went forward and led the people in the prayer. When he had finished the prayer, he addressed the people and said, "O people! Why did you start clapping when something happened to you in the prayer? Clapping is for women. Whenever one is confronted with something unusual in the prayer one should say, 'Subhan Allah'." Then the Prophet looked towards Abu Bakr and asked, "What prevented you from leading the prayer when I beckoned you to carry on?" Abu Bakr replied, "It does not befit the son of Al Quhafa to lead the prayer in the presence of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)
question: What is the significance of humility and respect in leadership roles within a religious context?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the significance of humility and respect in leadership roles, as Abu Bakr shows deference to the Prophet and prioritizes his presence over leading the prayer.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud:We used to say the greeting, name and greet each other in the prayer. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) heard it and said:--"Say, 'at-tahiyyatu lil-lahi was-salawatu wat-taiyibatu . Assalamu 'Alaika aiyuha-n-Nabiyu warahmatu- l-lahi wa-barakatuhu. _ Assalamu alaina wa-'ala 'ibadi-l-lahi as-salihin.. Ashhadu an la ilaha illa-l-lah wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan `Abdu hu wa Rasuluh. (All the compliments are for Allah and all the prayers and all the good things (are for Allah). Peace be on you, O Prophet, and Allah's mercy and blessings (are on you). And peace be on us and on the good (pious) worshipers of Allah. I testify that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is His slave and Apostle.) So, when you have said this, then you have surely sent the greetings to every good (pious) worshipper of Allah, whether he be in the Heaven or on the Earth
